summary: Add tab key moves focus to TextEdit

description.text:
Proposal: closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/3050

This PR adds a new option that lets you toggle the TextEdit/CodeEdit Tab trapping behavior. And a new shortcut to the Script/Shader editor to help out with keyboard navigation.

![Screenshot_20250208_205615](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e4343288-76fb-4e07-8bf5-257092bf4cf7)

![Screenshot_20250208_211344](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8614be22-c993-4264-b547-5404fcecab3d)

## Editor

### TextEdit
For TextEdit's used in the editor itself the setting has been set to true, going by this comment in the linked proposal:
> ...as most people won't be entering code in TextEdit nodes in projects.

Behavior can be changed by right clicking and changine the "Tab Key Moves Focus" option.

[Screencast_20250208_211957.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/06866aac-6f7a-48a1-85de-25a80f29c957)

## Script/Shader Editor
A new shortcut has been added (default Ctrl+M, same as [VS Code uses](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editor/accessibility#_tab-trapping))


[Screencast_20250208_221144.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2b295632-5e3f-4fbd-a22d-6dd18ddba9b7)

summary: InputEventScreenTouch gets propagated to a newly added scene

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproductible in v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Motoroal Edge Pro 20 - Android 13

### Issue description

When adding a new child scene by means of clicking a button, the InputEventScreenTouch event gets propagated to the newly added scene. The InputEventMouseButton event is not triggering the same behavior. Testing on PC with both Pointing options enabled (Emulate Touch From Mouse and Emulate Mouse From Touch) will not result in any problems, however when testing on my phone, I get the event propagated.

I think the InputEventScreenTouch event should not get propagated to a newly instantiated scene, that is not in the tree at the moment of the event generation (similar to the MouseButton event behavior).

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a main scene with 2 nodes: root Control and child Button
- Create a secondary scene with just a Control root
- In the main scene, add a script and connect the Button's pressed signal to the script. In the function, instantiate the secondary scene and add it as a child
- In the second scene, add a script and connect the Control gui_input signal to the script. In the fuction, match the event class and print the event
- Enable both Pointing options in the Project Settings and run the project on a PC and a mobile device to see the behavior difference


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Input Test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18734955/Input.Test.zip)

summary: [Editor] Prevent deferred tooltip update crash

description.text:
Caused by `TreeItem`s being accessed after clearing the tree on reset.

Can largely confirm this at least on 4.3, though without debug symbols I'm not sure if it is the same issue, can't reliably on 4.2, but hard to tell exactly why. I can reliably trigger it on debug builds, and with a few scenes in 4.4.beta3

Considered using `ObjectID` but I think this is a case where things should be solved by catching the problem at the source. This might still occur in edge cases where the timer fires during other updates, but this at least solves the issue reliably for me.

To trigger this bug:
* Open several scenes
* Edit the editor description of one of the root nodes
* Switch quickly between the tabs

The editor should crash, caused by various errors of memory access related to `TreeItem` nodes, this would be because the deferred tooltip update holding a reference to a now freed `TreeItem`, relevant code is:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/261e7d32d37445de3ef3a9804346552fdac6096e/editor/gui/scene_tree_editor.cpp#L642-L650
Where the timer set up, and:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/261e7d32d37445de3ef3a9804346552fdac6096e/editor/gui/scene_tree_editor.cpp#L657-L667
Where the crash is triggered, due to accessing internal data of a freed node

Ability to trigger this might vary based on hardware and general performance as this is a timing issue

summary: Window content_scale_factor causes jitter in Control size

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4-beta3, 4.3-stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS 24.04 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) UHD Graphics (CML GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10510U CPU @ 1.80GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Using the recommended way to scale UI to screen resolution by setting `get_tree().root.content_scale_factor` (either in the project settings or from script) causes Control node pixels to jitter on resizing the window. This is true even when the "Snap Controls to Pixels" option is enabled.

Interestingly, using the node's own scale factor (or scaling a parent node to rescale the entire UI) doesn't exhibit this behaviour, but causes a host of other issues with GUIs so it's not a possible workaround.

Screenshot of misaligned grid pixels from the MRP:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c906e43d-2bce-4ecd-b48a-aae6f9bb0015)

Screenshot of the grid looking perfect at a different resolution:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fb2dea18-5215-41d1-b84b-93b6cceea017)

I also managed to reproduce this in my game with all kinds of control nodes, including fonts. In that case the jitter is a lot more visible even when resizing at high resolutions.

**Update:**

Setting `content_scale_factor = 8.0` shows that this is not even a jitter, the control node's scale is just completely off when resizing to certain resolutions. There's no need for Nearest filtering either, the effect is clearly visible even on Linear filtered textures.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3cf1038a-1958-488d-acf5-fd5148140f8e

### Steps to reproduce

1. Place a control node (Confirmed with: TextureRect, Label).
3. Optionally, set the window resolution to a small value, such as 480x360. For me small window sizes (regardless of the base resolution) greatly exacerbate the effect, but it also happens from time to time on larger window sizes.
4. Set `display/window/stretch/scale` to a larger value, in my case 4.0 is enough, 8.0 makes it super visible.
5. Resize the game window by dragging the corner, and watch the control node size jitter.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[gui-scale.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18734113/gui-scale.zip)

summary: Game freezes over and over when playtesting

description.text:
### Tested versions

The project doesn't work in 4.2 so I couldn't test it there. This hasn't happened with any of my previous godot games, both of which were made in versions before 4.3.

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8] - Device name   DESKTOP-SO41CVE Processor    Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-7700 CPU @ 3.60GHz   3.60 GHz Installed RAM    12.0 GB Device ID    86E0BF9D-7A3D-4B61-9116-87BF6E4DCB7F Product ID    00325-96194-24947-AAOEM System type    64-bit operating system, x64-based processor Pen and touch    Pen support

### Issue description

Every now and again, when playtesting my game, the game (and my pc a bit too) will freeze every other second. (There's a video on this reddit post https://www.reddit.com/r/godot/comments/1hw99u2/my_godot_game_keeps_freezing_every_few_seconds/)

Sometimes it'll start happening when going to a new scene or loading a scene. Usually it'll just start happening immediately. It's also happened with a friend of mine's game that I playtested, which is 3D. My game is 2D. It also only happens on my computer, as I've uploaded the game to other computers and worked on it there and it's been fine. My friend with the 3D game has not had this issue at all, and friends that I send my game to for playtesting have never seen this issue. Also as I've said, it's extremely random. Some days it won't show up at all and others it wont go away no matter what. This makes it really hard to find a source for the issue.

I have tried:
- Reloading Godot. This works sometimes.
- Compressing or removing big textures. I can't tell if this helped or not.
- Simply waiting. Has worked like 3 times.
- Enabling and disabling v-sync in the game. Has also worked like 3 times.
- Switching the rendering mode to compatibility instead of forward+. I tried this for a little bit and the issue didn't come up, but I had to change it back to forward+ cause my game kinda needs it.
- Clearing shader cache. Did nothing.
- Downloading Godot from the website instead of Steam. Worked for a day or so.
- Playing the exported project. Did nothing.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/093ab928-ebce-45b2-a232-d22133d86f03)
(I'm not sure if this image works since I don't use github, but yeah nothing weird is happening with my gpu when it happens)

I also checked through the profiler and found nothing. The only thing it showed was a really long frame time when the freezes happened.

### Steps to reproduce

There aren't any set steps for reproducing it since it's random, I guess the best way is to try to load as many new scenes as possible or have a bunch of huge sprites in a scene.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Like I said, this issue does not happen when running/playtesting the game on other pcs, and it's not just with my game.

summary: Jolt Physics warnings for PhysicalBone3D fail to report bone names when running physics on separate thread

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4-beta2, 4.4-beta3
- Not reproducible in 4.3 (no Jolt implementation)

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Fedora Linux 41 (KDE Plasma) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 SUPER (nvidia; 565.77) - AMD Ryzen 7 8700F 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

I am using the native Jolt Physics engine on a separate thread via project settings (`physics/3d/run_on_separate_thread`). I have an armature that needs updating, so Jolt is emitting warnings. However, the warning is attempting to fetch the names of the problem bones, which is not thread-safe, so the warning text is malformed and is passed with multiple errors (as seen below).

```
ERROR: Caller thread can't call this function in this node (/root/@EditorNode@21257/@Panel@14/@VBoxContainer@15/DockHSplitLeftL/DockHSplitLeftR/DockHSplitMain/@VBoxContainer@26/DockVSplitCenter/@VSplitContainer@54/@VBoxContainer@55/@EditorMainScreen@102/MainScreen/@CanvasItemEditor@11479/@VSplitContainer@11131/@HSplitContainer@11133/@HSplitContainer@11135/@Control@11136/@SubViewportContainer@11137/@SubViewport@11138/RDMOutpost/world/renegade/visual/renegade/Skeleton3D/Physical Bone DEF-spine_001). Use call_deferred() or call_thread_group() instead.
   at: to_string (scene/main/node.cpp:2697)
ERROR: Caller thread can't call this function in this node (/root/@EditorNode@21257/@Panel@14/@VBoxContainer@15/DockHSplitLeftL/DockHSplitLeftR/DockHSplitMain/@VBoxContainer@26/DockVSplitCenter/@VSplitContainer@54/@VBoxContainer@55/@EditorMainScreen@102/MainScreen/@CanvasItemEditor@11479/@VSplitContainer@11131/@HSplitContainer@11133/@HSplitContainer@11135/@Control@11136/@SubViewportContainer@11137/@SubViewport@11138/RDMOutpost/world/renegade/visual/renegade/Skeleton3D/Physical Bone DEF-spine). Use call_deferred() or call_thread_group() instead.
   at: to_string (scene/main/node.cpp:2697)
WARNING: Hinge joint bias limit is not supported when using Jolt Physics. Any such value will be ignored. This joint connects '' and ''.
     at: set_param (modules/jolt_physics/joints/jolt_hinge_joint_3d.cpp:221)
```


When physics is set to run on the main thread, the problem resolves, showing the warnings as normal, including the bone names (expected behavior).
```
WARNING: Hinge joint bias limit is not supported when using Jolt Physics. Any such value will be ignored. This joint connects 'Physical Bone DEF-spine:<PhysicalBone3D#3027113173784>' and 'Physical Bone DEF-spine_001:<PhysicalBone3D#3027146728218>'.
     at: set_param (modules/jolt_physics/joints/jolt_hinge_joint_3d.cpp:221)
WARNING: Hinge joint bias limit is not supported when using Jolt Physics. Any such value will be ignored. This joint connects 'Physical Bone DEF-spine:<PhysicalBone3D#3102627426828>' and 'Physical Bone DEF-spine_001:<PhysicalBone3D#3102660981262>'.
     at: set_param (modules/jolt_physics/joints/jolt_hinge_joint_3d.cpp:221)
```

Should the warning itself be deferred to the main thread?

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set physics to run on a separate thread `physics/3d/run_on_separate_thread`
2. Switch Physics Engine to Jolt Physics
3. Set up a Skeleton3D with PhysicalBone3D bones that have joint properties unsupported in Jolt (i.e. a Pinjoint configured with impulse clamping)
4. Restart editor to see errors in console, or launch game to see them in debugger

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Warnings should appear as soon as this project is opened and imported.

[physicalbone3d_report_2025-02-09_17-35-51.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18726415/physicalbone3d_report_2025-02-09_17-35-51.zip)

summary: Fix global scripts not being added because of bad `global_script_class_cache.cfg`

description.text:
Fixes #102557, probably also fixes #102568

The Issue:
A [commit](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/318af42020cd349c5c9c3e4c88aa87249ca224b0) made "is_tool" and "is_abstract" mandatory to add new global classes to `ScriptServer::global_classes`.  But `global_script_class_cache.cfg` from projects before 4.4 lack these properties, so these classes won't be added, causing errors when running the game.

Current Solution:
After the editor scans all the scripts for the first time, now forcibly updates `global_script_class_cache.cfg`, resolving the issue. 
However, running a 4.3 game with 4.4 without first opening it with the editor at least once still fails.

I've tried to fix this in `ScriptServer::init_languages`, where the global classes are first loaded, but couldn't figure it out.

summary: Test locale is used on exported project

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in : v4.4.beta3.official [06acfccf8] , v4.4.beta2.official [a013481b0] , v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8] , v4.2.1.stable.official [b09f793f5]


### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1080 Ti (nvidia; 535.183.01) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i3-12100F (8 threads)

### Issue description

According to the documentation the settings parameter `internationalization/locale/test`

> If non-empty, this locale will be used when running the project from the editor.

From this follows that when running the exported project this setting will be ignored. But that is not what happens. The game will be using the locale set on `test`

### Steps to reproduce

- Open the MRP
- If your system is using the "zh" locale: go to the `internationalization/locale/test` setting and change it to "es"
- Export the game
- Run the exported game
- It should show the locale of your system, but will show the locale set in `internationalization/locale/test`

description.text:
- Fixes #102566

This PR reverts the frequency at which the update for the embedded process is performed. It is now executed again each time the floating window is resized or moved, significantly improving the user experience.

summary: Script Editor: Add option to disable documentation tooltips

description.text:
* See https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/91060#issuecomment-2646026349.
* This PR adds the setting `text_editor/behavior/documentation/enable_tooltips` that allows you to disable documentation tooltips for the Script Editor only (Inspector, Project Settings, etc. still show tooltips).
* In my opinion, this option makes sense regardless of the MacOS bugs, since some users may want to disable tooltips even if there are no bugs, if the tooltips annoy them. For Inspector, tooltips have the current behavior since 4.3.
* Simple debugger tooltips are shown if documentation tooltips are disabled. Partially reverts #101156 to this.

summary: Add Favorites to Asset Library

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->
(I messed up the git in another PR so I had to delete it)

This implements "favorite assets", based on https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/3521 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11080.

What this does:
- Adds a Star button to the right of AssetLibraryItems, toggling the Asset's favorite status.
- Adds a `Favorites...` button next to the search bar, opening a popup with the list of favorited assets.
- Stores "favorites" data in an editor setting `asset_library/favorites`, which is a Dictionary.
  - Keys are the asset_id as an int. Values are a Dictionary, caching the minimum info needed to spawn an AssetLibraryItem in the favorites list.
- Syncs the favorite status for the star toggle by listening to and emitting `settings_changed` in EditorSettings.
- Shows template_only assets only from the project manager, and the others only from the editor.

Images:

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/81e52894-f3c3-420f-8e1b-f391b9f24562)

- *Production edit: This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/3521.*

summary: Game Preview Freezes or Crashes on F5/F6 in Godot 4.4 Beta3 with Severe Window Ghosting

description.text:
**Issue Description**
When I press F5 or F6 to run the project, the Godot game preview window freezes. Sometimes, it crashes completely; other times, it does not, but I have to forcefully close it using the Task Manager.
Additionally, if the software does not crash, dragging the preview window leaves significant ghosting artifacts.

**Steps to Reproduce**
1,Create a Sprite2D node.
2.Add icon.svg as its texture.
3.Create a Node and name it main.
4.Attach a script (main.gd) to main with the following content:
```
extends Node
func _ready() -> void:
    var scene_resource = load("res://sprite.tscn")
    var root_node = scene_resource.instantiate()
    self.add_child(root_node)
func _process(delta: float) -> void:
    pass
```
5.Press F5 or F6 to run the project.
6.The preview window freezes or crashes. If it does not crash, dragging the window causes extreme ghosting.

**Godot Version**
Affected version: Godot 4.4 Beta3

**Comparison with Beta2:** 
The issue does not occur in Godot 4.4 Beta2, and there are no ghosting artifacts in that version.

summary: Maybe I'm asking too much of "match", but what about type safety?

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.3.stable
4.4.beta1
4.4.beta2

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot 4.4beta2

### Issue description

I was looking to be clever with `match` to simplify some parsing. But, it appears you cannot add types to the bound variables. This can cause some warnings (which are defaulted to off) to trigger. It's a shame to have bound variables be less useful in strict projects. Although, some warning_ignore statments can go a long way.

### Steps to reproduce

I thought I was being clever with my "match" guidelines. My cheat menu allows me to type something like "gem 5" and it will call that specific line of code. However, it spit up all sorts of warnings around method access to Variant types that I couldn't figure out how to get rid of. You cannot assign a type to the binding pattern arguments. In this case, the type could technically be inferred from the Array[String] type going in!

You can turn on all GDScript warnings in your script to see it in the below content.

```gdscript
static func apply_cheat(cheat: String) -> void:
	var regex: RegEx = RegEx.new()
	regex.compile("\\S+") # Negated whitespace character class.
	var results: Array[String] = []
	for result in regex.search_all(cheat):
		results.push_back(result.get_string())

	match results:
		["husk", var husks] when husks.is_valid_int():
			prints("add a husk", husks.to_int())
		["gem", var gems] when gems.is_valid_int():
			prints("add some gems", gems.to_int())
```


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any simple project.

summary: Enable changing editor language without restart

description.text:
Currently changing editor language requires editor restart. Godot has a good auto-translation system, which is under-utilized in the editor. For a long time the editor auto-translation was completely disabled. Then there is TTR method which we use everywhere, which makes it impossible to auto-translate, because it immediately translates the string.

#75012 enabled auto-translation to work in the editor, and recently there were some efforts to replace TTR with TTRC (e.g. #99158). Also in 4.4 there were various GUI enhancements that added better localization support to many Control nodes.

This PR makes another step - it actually enables language switching in the editor.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f8bf1a4-a8b4-437d-85ce-84f118ab63cf

Though as you can see, it's very basic right now. For proper language switching we need to use TTRC where possible, and NOTIFICATION_TRANSLATION_CHANGED where not (e.g. in format strings). Thus, while the PR makes changing the language setting have immediate effect, the user is still prompted to restart the editor as before.

IMO enabling this before the editor is ready is harmless, while it allows for easier testing of auto-translation. Eventually we will be able to properly switch editor language without restart at all.

Included some extra changes to the top menu bar, to make it more obvious that the language has changed.

summary: Upgrading project to 4.4 results in GDScript parser errors

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.beta3

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.26100 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 9 7940HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 Threads)

### Issue description

I can't quite explain this one, but I happened to run into this when reporting #102551.

In short, there's something strange going on with GDScript when upgrading this particular MRP from 4.3 to 4.4, where you'll be met with parser errors after upgrading, that then manifest themselves at runtime as well.

It seems that it has something to do with the stuff that's cached in the `.godot` folder, as deleting it resolves the errors, regardless if you delete it before or after upgrading. As such, I've included a more comprehensive set of reproduction steps to create the project from scratch, as opposed to providing an MRP.

I tried reducing down the script to something even more minimal, but for whatever reason this particular script seems to force the problem.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a new project using 4.3-stable
- Add a 2D main scene, call it `main.tscn`
- Add a new GDScript script through the FileSystem dock, call it `my_resource.gd`
- Populate `my_resource.gd` with the following contents:

```gdscript
extends Resource
class_name MyResource
```

- Attach a new script to the root of `main.tscn`, call it `main.gd`
- Populate `main.gd` with the following contents:

```gdscript
extends Node2D

@export var my_resource: MyResource

func _ready() -> void:
	bytes_to_var_with_objects(var_to_bytes_with_objects(my_resource)) as MyResource
```

- Run the main scene
- Note that it runs without errors
- Close the project
- Open the project in Godot 4.4-beta3
- Go through the upgrade process
- Note errors in Output pane saying:

```
ERROR: res://main.gd:3 - Parse Error: Could not find type "MyResource" in the current scope.
ERROR: res://main.gd:3 - Parse Error: Cannot use simple "@export" annotation because the type of the initialized value can't be inferred.
ERROR: res://main.gd:6 - Parse Error: Could not find type "MyResource" in the current scope.
ERROR: modules/gdscript/gdscript.cpp:3022 - Failed to load script "res://main.gd" with error "Parse error".
```

- Reload the project
- Note the lack of errors
- Run the main scene
- Note debugger break with error saying `Parser Error: Could not find type "MyResource" in the current scope.`
- Close the project
- Delete the `.godot` folder
- Open the project again
- Run the main scene
- Note how it runs fine

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Add a `run` SCons option to run the engine binary after a build

description.text:
This makes the compile-test cycle faster for engine contributors, as you no longer need to manually run the engine binary after it's done compiling. This can also avoid distractions that occur when you leave the engine building in the background and forget to run the project/editor afterwards.

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11719.

## Examples

```bash
# Run project manager.
scons run=-p

# This passes "yes" as a positional argument to Godot, which Godot won't do anything about, so it'll act the same as above unless CWD is a project folder.
scons run=yes

# Open a project in the editor.
# You can use `~` as a standin for the `$HOME` environment variable.
# This works on all platforms regardless of shell as this PR handles `~` specifically.
scons run=/path/to/project.godot

# Run a project directly.
scons run="--path /path/to/project"
```

## Preview

```
$ scons run="--path ~/Documents/Godot/test-tablet --quit"
scons: Reading SConscript files ...
Using SCons-detected MSVC version 14.3, arch x86_64
Building for platform "windows", architecture "x86_64", target "editor".
```

summary: Improve drag and drop into array property editors

description.text:
This makes a couple of things work that you'd expect to work:

- Dragging a node now highlights properties accepting that node's type as valid drop targets. The drop was already possible, it just wasn't indicated like it was for other types of properties.
- Custom resources can now be dropped onto array property editors that accept that resource.
- Nodes with a script can now be dropped onto array property editors that accept that type of script.
- Items can be added to an array property by dropping them on the `+ Add Element` button. The button is highlighted to indicate this.

Further details and rationale for implementation can be found in the individual commit messages.

Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/6862, which currently has 33 upvotes and no objections.

CC @YuriSizov who brought this up on Mastodon.

summary: push_warning/error show a leading 0 in the timestamp for the milliseconds

description.text:
### Tested versions

- v4.4.beta.gentoo [a013481b0]

### System information

Linux Gentoo Wayland

### Issue description

I was doing some good-ol' push_warning() debugging of my project and I was getting confused why some warnings were suddenly delayed by 900 msec. As it turns out, the Editor prints an additional zero when printing the timestamp.

Observed behaviour:
```
W 0:00:00:0990   main.gd:10 @ ticker(): TICK 37
W 0:00:01:0007   main.gd:10 @ ticker(): TICK 38
```

Expected behaviour:
```
W 0:00:00:990   main.gd:10 @ ticker(): TICK 37
W 0:00:01:007   main.gd:10 @ ticker(): TICK 38
```

The most likely culprit for this is in : `ScriptEditorDebugger::_parse_message` with the offending line being `String time = String("%d:%02d:%02d:%04d").sprintf(time_vals, &e);`


### Steps to reproduce

Do a bunch of push_warnings and/or push_errors and observe the millisecond field in the timestamp.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

* Create a new scene
* Attach the following script and run the scene

```gdscript
extends Node

func _ready() -> void:
	ticker()

func ticker() -> void:
	for i in range(1, 100):
		push_warning('TICK ', i)
		push_error('TICK ', i)
		await get_tree().physics_frame

	get_tree().quit()
```
